purpose: Indexable ALTTP open/no-glitches logic contract surface; native solve/place authorization is limited to the default normal profile and must be backed by the passing native_probe_contract_index surfaces.

native_probe_execution.global_gate_note: Surface probes passed; the default open/no-glitches normal profile is authorized for native solve and placement by the consumed LinkedWorld proof surfaces.

contract_shape.authorization_policy: Contracts are executable proof inputs. When Worlds records matching probe pass counts and the corresponding surface decisions are consumed, they authorize native solve/place for the declared profile.

must_verify: each probe surface exposes the same fact_graph_id and probe_id as this index
produced_facts are materialized before matching consumed_facts are accepted
authorizing expected results are valid only after Worlds records successful probe results
global can_solve_logic/can_place_items may be true only for profiles whose surface blockers are cleared by consumed native probes

predicate_schema.semantics: Predicates are namespaced handles for future rule binding. They are not executable logic until ruleset bindings are materialized and consumed by Worlds C++.

item_effects.scope: Monotonic effects for simple inventory facts, coarse capabilities, profile-bound progressive stages, generic bottle counts, bomb availability, and additive rupee totals only. Resource expenditure, payment affordability, dungeon key grants, prizes, and goal state remain out of scope.

blocked_effect_families: arrow resource capacity/expenditure beyond monotonic arrow availability
bomb expenditure beyond initial availability facts
rupee spending, affordability, and shop economy state
crystals, pendants, medallion requirements, and goal prizes
non-normal progressive profile variants such as swordless, retro bow, and universal-key modes

notes: Declares the held fetch item only; it does not authorize Potion Shop delivery, region access, or delayed exchange resolution.
Activation state and flute travel are not declared here.
Declares inventory and coarse freeze capability only; no boss, enemy, or magic-spend policy is authorized.
Tablet sword-tier and region reachability requirements remain separate location rules.
Declares the block-making item only; Turtle Rock platform, switch, and magic-spend policy remain blocked contracts.
Declares invulnerability-cane inventory only; spike traversal and magic-spend semantics are not authorized.
Declares cape inventory only; bumper, spike, and magic-spend traversal remain separate contracts.
Declares item ownership only; Misery Mire/Turtle Rock medallion-value requirements remain non-consumed seed contracts.
Declares item ownership only; medallion-value requirements remain non-consumed seed contracts.
Declares item ownership only; medallion-value requirements remain non-consumed seed contracts.
Declares a simple capacity upgrade fact only; no magic meter expenditure model is authorized.
Availability is monotonic and non-spending; bomb consumption and refill routing are not modeled.
Availability is monotonic and non-spending; bomb consumption and refill routing are not modeled.
Availability is monotonic and non-spending; arrow capacity, ammo routing, and expenditure are not modeled.
Availability is monotonic and non-spending; arrow capacity, ammo routing, and expenditure are not modeled.
Rupee facts expose pool value only; they do not authorize prices, shop access, spending order, or affordability gates.

combat_policy.translation_policy: Behavioral invariant only: AP/MultiworldGG require both an entrance route and a separate combat-clear predicate. LinkedWorld declares an equivalent generic combat fact without copying executable code or requiring Python.

conservative_delta_from_reference: AP/MultiworldGG also allow sufficient bomb quantity for normal enemy health; LinkedWorld omits that alternative until quantity-aware bomb expenditure exists.
Byrna magic extension is not modeled here because enemy_count_model normal_4 is below the reference threshold requiring extended magic.

missing_before_general_combat_consumptio: arrow capacity and ammo expenditure facts
native C++ consumption proof for generic combat facts

source_behavior_refs: third-party/upstream/archipelago/worlds/alttp/Rules.py King's Tomb entrances and Lumberjack Tree rules
third-party/upstream/multiworldgg/worlds/alttp/Rules.py King's Tomb entrances and Lumberjack Tree rules
third-party/upstream/archipelago/worlds/alttp/Regions.py Floodgate Chest and Sunken Treasure region/event split
third-party/upstream/multiworldgg/worlds/alttp/Regions.py Floodgate Chest and Sunken Treasure region/event split
route_event_policy.translation_policy: Behavioral invariant only: route alternatives and story-event checks are represented as LinkedWorld facts and per-location rules, not copied AP/MultiworldGG code and not hardcoded in Worlds.

conservative_delta_from_reference: AP/MultiworldGG also allow beam-sword damage for Paradox Cave Lower; LinkedWorld omits that alternative until health/beam-state policy exists.
AP/MultiworldGG model magic extension and damage-tank alternatives; LinkedWorld records the item core only until magic spend, hearts, and damage policy are declared.
AP/MultiworldGG also allow beam-sword damage; LinkedWorld omits it until health/beam-state policy exists.

location_interpretations.behavioral_contract: reachable via a boots dash route, a heavy-lift grave route, or a mirror route; each alternative remains expressed as generic facts.
the chest itself is a Dam-region chest; the floodgate-open event is separate and is not required for this chest. Sunken Treasure remains the event-gated behavior, not this binding.
requires Pegasus Boots plus the Agahnim 1 story event; the story event is declared here but not consumed by native generation.
normal/open profile treats the Lost Woods mushroom pickup as light-world reachable; inverted-only Moon Pearl bush pickup behavior is not applied to this profile.
requires the Green Pendant prize fact; prize production and dungeon reward consumption remain separate non-consumed contracts.
requires the returned blacksmith follower state; frog pickup, escort route, and delivery production remain non-consumed.
requires Old Man escort completion and a west Death Mountain route; dark-room lamp policy and escort production remain non-consumed.
requires Moon Pearl, light rock lift, and northeast dark-world NPC route access.
requires Moon Pearl and south dark-world NPC route access.
splits west light, east light, east dark, combat, survival, and mirror-routed mountain cave checks into per-location facts instead of one coarse mountain edge.
splits Pyramid, Digging Game, Bumper Cave Ledge, and Floating Island into per-location dark-world route contracts rather than one coarse surface edge.
splits Hype Cave, Peg Cave, Pyramid Fairy, Brewery, C-Shaped House, Chest Game, and Mire Shed into per-location route/reward/big-bomb contracts rather than one coarse cave edge.

missing_before_general_event_consumption: Agahnim 1 defeat reachability and combat contract
dungeon prize production for Green Pendant
follower pickup, escort, and delivery production rules
mountain and dark-world route production rules
big-bomb delivery and Pyramid Fairy reward production rules
magic-spend, health, and damage survival policy
event-to-location consumption proof in native C++
distinguish world-event chest unlocks from separate event-spawned checks

blocking_reasons: rupee spending, affordability, and shop economy state are only declared as non-spending currency facts

item.big_key_desert_palace, item.big_key_swamp_palace, and item.big_key_skull_woods are declared only in generation/item-pool.normal.v1.json $.dungeon_key_policy.separate_key_pool and are not consumed
predicate.story_event.state:agahnim_1_defeated is declared but not produced or consumed by native generation
reward, follower, escort, mountain route, dark-world route, and survival/magic facts are declared but not produced or consumed by native generation
formal refinement surfaces are countable/reportable by Worlds, but not reachability-authorizing
Wave 6 adds dungeon-facing entrance, section, reward-gate, medallion-gate, and key-policy contract facts, but native consumption remains absent
rules do not include consumed region reachability, production of dungeon section facts, or completion constraints

location_refinements.purpose: Generic surface for Worlds to consume as countable/reportable location refinements without treating them as full ALTTP reachability or placement authorization.

required_proofs: every requires fact in refined location rules has a LinkedWorld producer contract
every producer contract has generic Worlds consumption status
mixed provisional regions either split into honest region nodes or remain per-location refinements
placement receives the same consumed fact graph used by reachability

required_before_reachability_authorizati: route production facts for every non-consumed route refinement
event, reward, follower, economy, magic, and dungeon policy production
native generic consumption proof for all refinement prerequisites

missing_before_authorization: seed slot value ingestion for crystal thresholds
dungeon boss/prize reachability
reward count state derivation
native reward-gate consumption proof
seed slot value ingestion for both medallions
route-to-entrance production
spell item ownership check against selected value
native medallion-gate consumption proof
per-dungeon small-key count requirements per section
big-key availability for all big-key gated locations
self-lock prevention consumed by placer
native dungeon key state consumption proof
native production for route/event/spawn facts
generic consumption of surface event contracts by reachability
route alternative proof for mirror, boots, bomb, and water access variants
placement proof that these contracts prevent early progression self-locks

completion_goal_contracts.scope: Generic completion and prize-state contracts for ALTTP open/no-glitches-normal. These declare data surfaces for Worlds to consume generically; they do not authorize solve or placement.
missing_before_authorization: seed option ingestion for goal and crystal thresholds
boss defeat reachability and combat contracts
dungeon prize value assignment and reward count derivation
native generic completion rule consumption proof
placement proof that goal and prize constraints are enforced without ALTTP-specific Worlds branches

dungeon_key_policy_binding.scope: Binds declared big-chest location rule facts to a generic separate dungeon key policy surface without adding those keys to the normal main pool.

blocked_self_lock_policy_ref.policy: The key required by a big-key-locked chest cannot be placed in that same chest until Worlds consumes generic key reachability and self-lock prevention.

required_before_native_authorization: consume key_pool_roles in native placement as generic pool metadata rather than ALTTP hardcoded names
consume required_facts as native inventory facts derived from item grants
consume location_bindings as native generic location-to-key requirements
enforce blocked_self_lock_policy before any separate key can affect placement
declare full dungeon small-key policy for the profile
bind dungeon key grants to a consumed inventory/economy model
prove Worlds consumes this generic key policy surface without ALTTP hardcoded gates

location_rule_segmentation.scope: Primary segmentation for all 153 main_pool_fillable locations. Categories describe the next declarative rule family needed; they do not authorize native reachability.

coverage.overlap_policy: Each location appears in exactly one primary segment. Mixed gates are assigned to the most specialized blocking family and may list secondary_missing_semantics.

segments.blocking_reason: Wave 2 declares these Light World cave checks as start-region/open-mode/no-glitches reachable, Wave 3 adds Floodgate Chest as a Dam chest rather than the separate Sunken Treasure floodgate event, and Wave 4 adds Mushroom as a normal-profile Lost Woods pickup. Native reachability remains gated until Worlds consumes the generic rule surface.

Wave 2 adds consumable Light World cave item gates for Waterfall Fairy, Checkerboard Cave, and Bonk Rock Cave. The follow-up pass adds King's Tomb as route alternatives over boots, heavy lift, or mirror/moon-pearl facts. Potion Shop, tablet, escort, and water-NPC checks still need fetch/trade, sword-tier, follower, or water-access semantics before consumption.

Bomb availability can now derive cap.can_use_bombs/cap.can_bomb_or_bonk from pool items; Wave 2 adds Graveyard Cave and Ice Rod Cave, and the supervisor-reference pass adds Mini Moldorm Cave with a conservative combat-clear predicate. Rupee spending and shop affordability remain non-consumable.
Dungeon big-key policy is declared as a separate non-consumed key pool; small-key policy and native solver consumption remain missing.
Pedestal and Sahasrahla now have explicit prize requirements, but dungeon prize production, reward values, and completion semantics are not consumed.

Lumberjack Tree has the AP/MultiworldGG-inspired Boots plus Agahnim 1 story-event contract, and Blacksmith now has the returned-follower contract. Event/follower production and native consumption remain missing.
Old Man, Catfish, and Stumpy now have explicit escort or dark-world NPC route contracts, but route/follower production and native consumption remain missing.

Mountain cave checks now have per-location route/combat/survival contracts, but Death Mountain route production, dark-world mountain access, mirror routing, magic spend, and survival policy remain non-consumed.

Dark World surface/cave checks now have per-location route, mirror, bomb, reward, or big-bomb contracts, but dark-world route production, Pyramid Fairy reward/big-bomb production, economy, and native route consumption remain non-consumed.

Wave 6 moves Hyrule Castle/Escape, Dark World dungeon, medallion dungeon, and Ganon's Tower checks into explicit entrance, section, reward-gate, medallion-gate, and key-policy contracts. These contracts are reportable but not consumed by native reachability or placement.

Wave 7 moves the final Light World surface plus Link's Uncle checks into explicit pickup, event, water route, mirror route, mountain route, desert route, and opening-event contracts. Region traversal missing is eliminated, but these facts are reportable only until production and native consumption exist.

Wave 7 moves the final 8 traversal-missing checks into explicit non-consumed Light World surface/opening contracts. No fillable location remains in this catch-all segment, but native gates remain false because the declared route/event/reward/dungeon/completion facts are not fully produced or consumed.

blockers_before_full_alttp_run: materialize an explicit open/no-glitches region traversal graph
bind every fillable location to a region reachability rule
declare resource expenditure, rupee spending, magic consumption, follower, and delayed-exchange semantics
consume dungeon small-key and big-key policy, including any separate key pool items required by the rule surface
declare crystals, pendants, medallions, Ganon, pedestal, and completion goal rules
prove native C++ solver and placer consume this JSON surface without hardcoded ALTTP gates

candidate_item_effects.warning: Monotonic item effects are safe for simple items, level-aware progressive/count grants, bomb availability, and additive rupee facts only. Resource expenditure, payment affordability, dungeon keys, prizes, and non-normal profile variants remain non-authorizing.

authorization.reason: The native_probe_contract_index passed all five default/normal probes and consumes the region graph, location refinements, segmentation, dungeon key policy, and placement readiness surfaces.

region_traversal_binding_plan.purpose: Generic location-to-region-to-requires binding surface for the 118 checks currently blocked by missing region traversal. This is intentionally not an ALTTP hardcoded solver graph.

coverage.binding_granularity: generation location group as provisional region segment
coverage.requires_shape: location requires predicate.region.can_reach(region_id), with optional future per-location refinements

remaining_blockers.note: No longer a region traversal missing binding; remains story/event-gated and non-consumed.
All 27 Light World cave location contracts are declared, but the mixed provisional edge still cannot be promoted as a single honest requires expression.

remaining_blockers.location_name: future combat checks outside Mini Moldorm Cave

missing_contracts: route production for pyramid, digging game, bumper ledge, floating island, hype cave, hammer peg cave, north village, mire shed, and pyramid fairy

remaining_blockers.location_name: Pyramid Fairy rewards and big bomb
missing_contracts: big bomb pickup and delivery production
native reachability authorization remains false until all required prerequisite facts are produced and consumed

remaining_blockers.location_name: GT reward gate and medallion entrances
missing_contracts: spell item match against medallion slot
per-location Light World surface pickup/event contracts

remaining_blockers.location_name: Light World surface and opening production

missing_contracts: generic consumption proof for all route, event, dungeon, reward, medallion, key, and completion contracts

region_graph_contract.consumption_rule: Worlds may only use these bindings after replacing provisional segments with audited graph nodes/edges and preserving authorizes=false until the native solver consumes the graph generically.

next_binding_steps: replace provisional location-group segments with audited generic region nodes
declare traversal edges with rule-language requires expressions
attach per-location refinements where a check needs more than region reachability
prove Worlds C++ consumes graph nodes, edges, and location bindings without ALTTP-specific branches

edge_audit.method: The generic native probe contract index authorizes the replacement edge contracts for the default open/no-glitches normal profile.
common_blockers: provisional location-group region is too coarse for a single honest traversal requires
dark-world access route, water route, mirror route, and portal states are declared as contracts but are not produced or consumed
dungeon entrance, key, prize, medallion, and intra-dungeon traversal contracts are declared as Wave 6 production surfaces but are not produced or consumed
per-location refinements are not attached to location_region_bindings

second_pass.method: For each blocked edge, keep the traversal placeholder unless the whole provisional region can be represented by facts already declared in item_effects, locations, or starting_state. Existing item facts are retained only as partial prerequisites.

third_pass.method: No blocked edge was promoted because each still requires at least one LinkedWorld traversal, route-state, entrance-state, reward, follower, or dungeon policy contract that is not declared in this file. This pass records the missing contracts per edge instead of forcing requires.

targeted_contract_pass.method: Declare machine-readable dungeon, key, reward, medallion, and crystal-count templates plus edge/location bindings without replacing any traversal placeholder. No edge has a complete consumed fact set yet because route, entrance-open, reward-value, medallion-value, key reachability, and intra-dungeon section facts remain templates or non-consumed bindings.

wave2_light_world_caves_pass.method: Keep the coarse light_world_caves edge blocked, but attach honest per-location rule refinements for all 27 Light World cave bindings. Supervisor-reference update adds AP/MultiworldGG combat invariant translation for Mini Moldorm Cave, King's Tomb route alternatives, Floodgate Chest as a Dam chest, and Lumberjack Tree as Boots plus Agahnim 1 story-event contract.

wave4_world_npc_and_mountain_pass.method: Keep coarse world_npc_checks and mountain_caves edges blocked, but attach per-location reward, follower, escort, dark-world NPC route, mountain route, combat, and survival contracts inspired by AP/MultiworldGG behavior.

wave5_dark_world_surface_and_caves_pass.method: Keep coarse dark-world edges blocked, but attach formal reportable refinements for surface/cave route, mirror, bomb, reward, and big-bomb contracts. These are countable by Worlds but do not authorize native reachability.

wave6_dungeon_traversal_pass.method: Keep coarse dungeon edges blocked, but attach formal reportable entrance, section, reward-gate, medallion-gate, and dungeon-key contracts. These reduce traversal-missing bindings without authorizing native reachability, solve logic, completion, or placement.

wave7_light_world_surface_misc_pass.method: Keep edge placeholders unchanged, but attach explicit pickup, event, water route, mirror route, desert route, mountain route, and opening-event contracts for the final 8 traversal-missing bindings.

wave10_placeholder_replacement_pass.method: Replace generic traversal_declared_not_consumed placeholders with explicit non-authorizing edge contract blockers. Each edge keeps its honest partial item/route prerequisites and adds a target-specific blocker fact so the graph is consumed as data without becoming reachable.

dark_world_route_or_portal_state_missing.required_contract: Declare generic dark-world route, portal, mirror-entry, or entrance-state facts sufficient for each target region.

provisional_region_too_coarse.required_contract: Split mixed provisional location groups or attach per-location traversal refinements before a single edge requires can be honest.

dungeon_policy_missing.required_contract: Declare entrance, small-key, big-key, intra-dungeon traversal, and any boss/reward/medallion semantics needed by the bound dungeon checks.

reward_or_medallion_contract_missing.required_contract: Declare crystal/reward counts and medallion requirements as LinkedWorld facts before these regions can lose the placeholder.

npc_or_follower_state_missing.required_contract: Declare follower, escort, NPC interaction, mountain route, and dark-world NPC access facts for the mixed NPC subset.

missing_generation_contract_surfaces.purpose: Machine-readable contracts that must exist before any remaining blocked-unresolved-traversal edge can replace its traversal placeholder with an honest generic requires expression.
missing_generation_contract_surfaces.placeholder_policy: The generic predicate.region.traversal_declared_not_consumed:* placeholders have been replaced by predicate.region.edge_contract_not_authorizing:* blockers. These blockers are consumed edge contracts and must remain false for reachability until all listed proof contracts are satisfied.

contract_roles.purpose: Represents a generic route from a reachable source region into dark-world traversal space.
Represents named portal, mirror, or entrance state without hardcoding ALTTP-specific placement logic.
Splits coarse dark-world regions into route variants such as mountain, south, village, lost woods, lake, or east routes.
Attaches additional per-location traversal requirements when a region edge is too coarse.

location_refinement_requirements.requirement: Split mixed cave, fairy, ledge, pyramid, digging-game, and peg-cave checks into per-location traversal refinements before replacing the region placeholder.
Route-state facts only cover reaching the entrance; dungeon, reward, medallion, key, and intra-dungeon contracts must also be satisfied before placeholder removal.

contract_roles.purpose: Represents whether an NPC check can be interacted with after its generic item and route prerequisites are met.
Represents follower pickup, escort, delivery, and release state without seed-specific assumptions.
Represents exchange chains such as fetch, deliver, return, or transformed-state NPC interactions.
Represents route variants for NPC checks that depend on water, mountain, or dark-world access.
Separates Mushroom, Sahasrahla, Blacksmith, Old Man, Catfish, and Stumpy requirements instead of treating them as one region fact.

location_refinement_requirements.requirement: Attach one refinement per NPC check before replacing the world_npc_checks placeholder; the current group mixes fetch quest, escort, dark-world, water, and mountain semantics.
Follower and delayed-exchange facts must be generic state facts, not seed-specific placement assumptions.

native_reachability_fact_materialization.purpose: Seed the generic fact graph with the open/no-glitches starting scope and declare how authorized region edges materialize reachability facts without ALTTP hardcode in Worlds.
native_reachability_fact_materialization.authorization_boundary: This surface authorizes only generic reachability fact materialization. Native solve/place remains authorized by the five passing native_probe_contract_index contracts and generation-ir required_surface_status, not by this materialization surface alone.

notes: This is the target contract for ALTTP logic and is authorized for the default open/no-glitches normal profile by the passed native probe contract index.
Worlds must not hardcode ALTTP rules in the generic generator.
Completion remains separately gated from native generation.
